name: Old Fashioned Fruit Cake #3
recipeInstructions:
Preparation: Instead of watermelon rind preserves, I used peach and strawberries as a substitute. This is an old recipe written by my mama (Clara Norman McCulley). The temperature wasn't mentioned because the wood stoves didn't have thermostats. She used the ingredients she had at home. She cooked in a big black iron skillet lined with wax paper, so do I. You can also use the pound cake pan with a hole in the center. Bake at 250 degrees.
1. Mix all wet ingredients (butter, sugar, molasses, juice, buttermilk, jam, preserves) in a large bowl.
2. Add flour, pecans, soda, eggs, apples, salt (if using), raisins, pineapple, cherries, and cocoa to the wet mixture. Stir until combined.
3. Divide the batter into three equal parts and bake each in separate skillets or pans at 250 degrees for the desired time.
4. Wrap the cooled cakes in towels and store them in an old pie safe on the back porch.
5. Just before Christmas, slice an apple and place it around the cakes, then add some blackberry wine for aroma.
